acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a bounded first-pass role/domain lens decision for the ADF successor line that is explicitly tested against the shallow surface map slice.
The test result chooses an initial lens without pretending the lifetime ADF identity is final.
completion_signals: A root-level test note compares a few plausible ADF lenses against the first slice and recommends one initial fit.
The resulting recommendation is strong enough to carry into the successor creation request.
escalation_conditions: The lens discussion drifts into a long catalog or identity debate before the first slice exists.
The chosen lens cannot explain why it improves the shallow surface map over a generic research posture.
tasks.summary: Run the first bounded ADF role/domain lens test against the shallow surface map slice and choose the initial line-specific framing to carry into the creation request.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a concrete first-slice definition for the ADF successor line that stays shallow, read-only, and early-reviewable.
The slice definition names the exact artifact classes, stop conditions, and follow-on path without slipping back into open-ended ADF exploration.
completion_signals: A root-level spec exists for the ADF successor shallow surface map first slice.
The spec identifies the slice question, input classes, output artifacts, acceptance criteria, and stop conditions.
escalation_conditions: The first slice definition expands into deep dependency theory, predictive analytics, or broad publication work before producing one shallow machine-readable map.
The first slice cannot state what artifact counts as success and therefore risks another long blind build phase.
tasks.summary: Define the first ADF successor slice as a shallow read-only surface map that produces one machine-readable map and one operator-reviewable summary before any deeper dependency or playbook work.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a bounded creation-planning path for the fresh ADF successor line that uses the shallow surface map as wave 1.
The creation-planning path names the role/domain choice, personality choice if any, agent-native profile, and first-wave objective without overcommitting later layers.
completion_signals: A template-creation or materialization request shape exists for the new ADF successor line.
The request shape carries the first-wave shallow-surface-map objective explicitly and keeps later dependency, playbook, and predictive layers out of the first request.
escalation_conditions: The proposed request tries to encode the whole ADF dream into the first line instead of one bounded proof loop.
The request cannot distinguish template-level reusable framing from operator- or line-specific runtime ambition.
tasks.summary: Prepare the bounded creation-planning request for the fresh ADF successor line, using the shallow surface map as the explicit first-wave proof slice.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory executes the prepared ADF successor template-creation request successfully and records the new template line with the intended agent-native and personality defaults.
The resulting template is ready for first materialization without pulling later-wave ADF ambitions into the creation step.
completion_signals: The prepared ADF successor template-creation request is executed through the standard PackFactory workflow.
Registry and template-local evidence make the new successor template line discoverable.
escalation_conditions: Executing the prepared request would require unsupported inline custom role/domain data that current request schemas do not admit.
The template-creation step starts absorbing first-wave implementation work instead of staying a clean line-creation step.
tasks.summary: Execute the prepared template-creation request for the fresh ADF successor line and register the new template as the bounded successor baseline.

acceptance_criteria: The PackFactory root has schema-valid objective, backlog, and work-state files.

completion_signals: contracts/project-objective.json, tasks/active-backlog.json, and status/work-state.json exist at the factory root.
tools/refresh_factory_autonomy_memory.py and the root docs acknowledge the root tracker.
escalation_conditions: The shared control-plane schemas prove too build-pack-specific to represent PackFactory root work cleanly.
tasks.summary: Seed the PackFactory root objective, backlog, and work-state files and wire root tooling to use them.

acceptance_criteria: The operator dashboard surfaces portfolio state, environment assignments, recent factory motion, and root task guidance clearly enough to replace long routine startup briefs.
The dashboard remains local-first and keeps the Python snapshot generator as the truth-preserving data-preparation layer.
completion_signals: The dashboard specs and implementation path align on the root executive-summary surface.
Routine PackFactory status checks no longer need a long chat-only reconstruction path.
escalation_conditions: A dashboard change would weaken the canonical registry-first truth model or create conflicting operator views.
tasks.summary: Advance the top-priority operator dashboard so it becomes the fast normal briefing surface for PackFactory state.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory can declare whether a build-pack is local-factory-managed or remote-managed without leaving dual-primary ambiguity.

acceptance_criteria: The agent-native initialization spec states that v1 is declaration-only and optional.

acceptance_criteria: The tracker/planner formalization spec keeps planning context advisory and tracker-backed only.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a reusable personality-template model that stays optional and does not collapse template reuse.

PackFactory tooling can inherit, override, or clear the role/domain overlay the same way it already can for personality.
escalation_conditions: A proposed role/domain system would blur into personality so heavily that the two overlays stop being distinguishable.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit root-level tech spec for a shared headless-browser proof tool that validates real browser behavior on operator-facing preview surfaces without turning that capability into a build-pack.
The spec keeps the tool bounded, aligned with the factory testing policy, and explicit that the first usage is on-demand proof rather than a broad default gate.
The spec names at least one concrete proving-ground case, such as the ADF field-manual step-expansion behavior.
completion_signals: A factory-root browser-proof spec exists under `docs/specs/project-pack-factory/` and is tied to one named root planner task.
The spec clearly distinguishes shared PackFactory tool runtime from build-pack content or build-pack-local helpers.
The next implementation pass can build a wrapper tool without re-arguing whether the capability belongs at the factory root.
escalation_conditions: The proposed tool would effectively become a broad new default UI test suite rather than a bounded proof surface.
The browser runtime model cannot be kept outside canonical build-pack truth surfaces or would create persistent tool-state ambiguity.
tasks.summary: Design a shared PackFactory headless-browser proof tool so agents can validate real operator-facing browser behavior on preview surfaces without turning that capability into a build-pack or a broad default UI test suite.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a shared root wrapper tool that implements the bounded `adf_field_manual_hash_target_opens` proof recipe without becoming a raw browser-framework passthrough.

The wrapper keeps browser runtime state under `.pack-state/browser-proof-runtime/`, proof reports under `.pack-state/browser-proofs/`, and remains read-only against canonical factory control-plane state.
The tool emits schema-valid request and report artifacts, proves the ADF field-manual interaction when the host can launch Chromium, and otherwise fails closed with explicit host-dependency evidence.
completion_signals: `tools/run_browser_proof.py` exists with a narrow PackFactory-facing CLI for the ADF proving-ground recipe.
The request and report schema pair exists under `docs/specs/project-pack-factory/schemas/` and the tool validates against them.
A real proof run against the ADF preview generates an auditable report under `.pack-state/browser-proofs/`, even when the host blocks browser launch.
escalation_conditions: The implementation would require turning the wrapper into a raw Playwright passthrough or a default validation gate.
The runtime cannot stay isolated under `.pack-state/` or the report surface cannot stay supplementary instead of canonical control-plane state.
tasks.summary: Implement the shared PackFactory browser-proof wrapper and schema pair for the bounded ADF field-manual proving-ground recipe.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit adjacent spec that defines the host-level shared-library dependency boundary for the root browser-proof wrapper and makes clear that these packages are host prerequisites rather than build-pack content.

The spec records a bounded readiness check path that can prove whether a host can launch the Playwright Chromium runtime before operators interpret browser-proof failures as PackFactory logic problems.
The current blocker is backed by recorded evidence from the live browser-proof report plus a direct shared-library inspection of the downloaded Chromium binary.
completion_signals: A root planner task exists for the host dependency follow-up and points at the adjacent spec and evidence note.
The adjacent spec names the missing shared libraries, the provisioning boundary, and the rerun path for the existing ADF proving-ground request.
The evidence note links the schema-valid fail-closed report to the direct `ldd` output so the blocker is auditable outside chat.
escalation_conditions: A proposed fix would push host package provisioning into build-packs or otherwise blur the host-versus-factory boundary.
A proposed fix would weaken the fail-closed proof model by hiding missing host libraries behind a fallback browser path.
tasks.summary: Define the host dependency provisioning and readiness-check path for the root browser-proof wrapper so Chromium launch blockers stay explicit and operationally actionable.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a bounded root-level model for agent-native project initialization that stays declaration-only in v1 and does not create a second execution control plane.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has an explicit authority rule that keeps planner context advisory and tracker state canonical.

acceptance_criteria: The root backlog and work-state use one stable set of task ids and summaries for the agent-native initialization initiative without draft-versus-final naming drift.

acceptance_criteria: PackFactory has a clear backup and git strategy that distinguishes what should be committed, what should be backed up outside git, and what should remain local-only or reconstructible.
